Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Specialized 4′-deoxyflavone/root-specific flavone (RSF) biosynthetic pathway; parallel flavone branch of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Metabolic engineering target for production of bioactive flavones; may enable increased chrysin/4′-deoxyflavone production in medicinal plants or heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the C2=C3 double bond in flavanones to form flavones; converts pinocembrin to chrysin and also converts naringenin to apigenin and eriodictyol to luteolin in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase requiring NADPH; assays used NADPH and yeast strain coexpressing Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1; reduced glutathione included in assay buffer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad-specificity flavone synthase activity, but silencing had no detectable effect on major root-specific flavones in hairy roots, indicating it is not the main determinant of RSF accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Scutellaria hairy roots and organ-specific tissues; MeJA treatment of hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Yeast microsomes expressing SbFNSII-1 produced chrysin from pinocembrin, apigenin from naringenin, and luteolin from eriodictyol; products matched authentic standards by retention time and MS; reaction required NADPH
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Acts at the flavanone-to-flavone conversion step, but RNAi did not reduce baicalin, wogonoside, baicalein, or wogonin, placing it outside the specialized root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone branch despite catalytic competence
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq contig screening for FNS/CYP93B homologs from hairy roots followed by 3′/5′ RACE and RT-PCR cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Independent transgenic hairy root RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC; LC-MS/MS; yeast microsome enzyme assay kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Likely retained ancestral broad FNSII activity; preferred substrate is naringenin based on Vmax/Km; expressed at relatively low levels across organs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No detectable change in the four measured RSFs upon RNAi silencing
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General/classic flavone branch and possible overlap with 4′-deoxyflavone branch at flavanone oxidation step
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP93B family; membrane-associated FNSII
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Km 0.24 μM and Vmax 27.65 pkat mg protein−1 for pinocembrin; Km 0.28 μM and Vmax 60.93 pkat mg protein−1 for naringenin; RNAi line with 9.6% transcript still showed no significant RSF reduction
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Hairy roots; roots, stems, leaves, flowers; yeast microsomes

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Specialized 4′-deoxyflavone/root-specific flavone (RSF) biosynthetic pathway; parallel flavone branch of phenylpropanoid/flavonoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: High-value metabolic engineering target to enhance production of medicinal 4′-deoxyflavones such as chrysin, baicalein, wogonin and their glycosides in Scutellaria or heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specifically introduces the C2=C3 double bond in pinocembrin to form chrysin; does not accept 4′-hydroxylated flavanones such as naringenin or eriodictyol
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase requiring NADPH; yeast host coexpressed Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for accumulation of major root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones; promotes flux from pinocembrin to chrysin and downstream baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided cloning, qRT-PCR, MeJA response, RNAi silencing in hairy roots, recombinant yeast microsome assay, in vivo yeast feeding, kinetic analysis, Arabidopsis overexpression with substrate feeding, transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Root-specific flavone production in hairy roots and roots; induced in hairy roots by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Yeast microsomes expressing SbFNSII-2 converted only pinocembrin to chrysin, verified by retention time and MS against authentic standard; no product formed without NADPH; in vivo yeast feeding produced chrysin from pinocembrin but not apigenin from naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: RNAi caused strong reduction in baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin and accumulation of pinocembrin-O-glucuronide/dihydrobaicalin derivative/dihydrowogonin, showing that SbFNSII-2 functions at the pinocembrin-to-chrysin step upstream of decorated RSFs; Arabidopsis expressing SbFNSII-2 produced chrysin only when fed pinocembrin; pathway reconstruction in tobacco confirmed role
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq contig screening for FNS/CYP93B homologs from hairy roots followed by RT-PCR cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Independent hairy root RNAi lines; independent Arabidopsis transgenic lines; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM methyl jasmonate for 24 h; pinocembrin feeding in Arabidopsis and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC; LC-MS/MS/MS2; yeast microsome enzyme assays; metabolite profiling in transgenic Arabidopsis and infiltrated tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Likely neofunctionalized after recent duplication from SbFNSII-1; root-enriched expression; lower catalytic activity than SbFNSII-1 but strict substrate specificity for pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Accumulation of pinocembrin-O-glucuronide, dihydrobaicalin-O-glucuronide, and dihydrowogonin in RNAi lines
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP93B family; membrane-associated FNSII
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Root transcript level 8-, 28-, and 36-fold higher than stem, leaf, and flower, respectively; MeJA increased transcript 4.8-fold; RNAi line 1 with 82% transcript reduction showed 71% and 65% reductions in baicalin and wogonoside; RNAi line 7 with 4.6% transcript retained only 17.9%, 18.4%, and 5.2% of control baicalin, wogonoside, and baicalein; Arabidopsis lines accumulated 2.18–3.79 mg g−1 DW chrysin when fed pinocembrin; kinetic parameters for pinocembrin: Km 0.46 μM, Vmax 9.02 pkat mg protein−1
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Chrysin and downstream root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones including baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, wogonin
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Accumulation/content and required enzymatic conversion from pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Hairy roots; roots; Arabidopsis seedlings; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Arabidopsis ecotype Columbia used for heterologous validation

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway and related CoA activation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Limited direct value for 4′-deoxyflavone engineering; useful comparator enzyme distinguishing general 4CL from RSF-specific cinnamate ligase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids and cinnamic acid by ligating coenzyme A to form corresponding CoA esters; most efficient with 4-coumaric acid and caffeic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a general 4-coumarate/caffeate CoA ligase rather than an RSF-specific cinnamate ligase; not directly validated as determinant of root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided cloning, phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR expression profiling, recombinant His-tag protein purification in E. coli, spectrophotometric enzyme assay, kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Organ-specific expression in roots, stems, leaves, flowers; MeJA treatment of hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified recombinant SbCLL-1 converted cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, and caffeic acid in standard 4CL assays; kinetic preference for 4-coumaric and caffeic acids
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Phylogenetic grouping with Arabidopsis 4CL1/2/3/5 and expression in stems/roots support role in general phenylpropanoid metabolism rather than the specialized cinnamoyl-CoA supply step for RSFs
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq contig screening for 4CL/CLL homologs from hairy roots followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Spectrophotometric CoA ligase assay at 311/333/363 nm; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Highest expression in stems followed by roots; likely more associated with lignin/general phenylpropanoid metabolism; discussed as analogous to At4CL1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert cinnamic acid toward non-RSF phenylpropanoid products
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Km/Vmax/Vmax/Km: cinnamic acid 5300 μM/180 nkat mg−1/0.03; 4-coumaric acid 45 μM/536/11.88; caffeic acid 18 μM/190/10.58; transcript significantly enhanced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Precursor activation potential relative to RSF branch
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Roots, stems, leaves, flowers; recombinant enzyme assays

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid/flavonoid pathway and related CoA activation reactions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Limited direct value for 4′-deoxyflavone engineering; useful comparator enzyme distinguishing general 4CL from RSF-specific cinnamate ligase activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids and cinnamic acid by ligating coenzyme A to form corresponding CoA esters; highest efficiency with 4-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: General 4CL-like enzyme, not demonstrated as a specialized driver of RSF synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided cloning, phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR expression profiling, recombinant His-tag protein purification in E. coli, spectrophotometric enzyme assay, kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Organ-specific expression in roots, stems, leaves, flowers; MeJA treatment of hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified recombinant SbCLL-5 converted cinnamic acid, 4-coumaric acid, and caffeic acid in standard 4CL assays; kinetic preference strongest for 4-coumaric acid
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Phylogenetic grouping with canonical Arabidopsis 4CLs and broad substrate use support a general phenylpropanoid role rather than dedicated cinnamoyl-CoA supply for RSFs
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq contig screening for 4CL/CLL homologs from hairy roots followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Spectrophotometric CoA ligase assay at 311/333/363 nm; kinetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Expressed highly in roots and stems; structurally likened to At4CL3 and with similar affinity for 4-coumarate
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert precursor flux toward non-RSF phenylpropanoid products
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Km/Vmax/Vmax/Km: cinnamic acid 3060 μM/279 nkat mg−1/0.09; 4-coumaric acid 31 μM/687/22.15; caffeic acid 260 μM/600/2.31; transcript significantly enhanced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Precursor activation potential relative to RSF branch
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Roots, stems, leaves, flowers; recombinant enzyme assays

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Specialized 4′-deoxyflavone/root-specific flavone (RSF) biosynthetic pathway; cinnamoyl-CoA supply branch from cinnamic acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Key metabolic engineering target for increasing flux into medicinal 4′-deoxyflavones by bypassing C4H and supplying cinnamoyl-CoA; valuable for heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specifically ligates coenzyme A to cinnamic acid to form cinnamoyl-CoA; no detectable activity with 4-coumaric acid or caffeic acid
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Requires CoA, ATP, and Mg2+; contains C-terminal SKL peroxisomal targeting motif
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for efficient accumulation of major root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones by supplying cinnamoyl-CoA for pinocembrin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq-guided cloning, phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR expression profiling, recombinant protein purification and kinetics, RNAi silencing in hairy roots, Arabidopsis overexpression, transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Root-enriched expression in S. baicalensis; hairy roots; Arabidopsis seedlings supplied with cinnamic acid; transient expression in tobacco leaves with or without cinnamic acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Purified recombinant SbCLL-7 converted only cinnamic acid in CoA ligase assays and displayed far better catalytic efficiency for cinnamic acid than SbCLL-1 or SbCLL-5
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: RNAi reduced baicalin, wogonoside, and baicalein; expression is root-enriched and coincident with RSF accumulation; coexpression with SbCHS-2 in tobacco generated pinocembrin, and with SbFNSII-2 generated chrysin, placing SbCLL-7 upstream of pinocembrin and chrysin formation
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq contig screening for 4CL/CLL homologs from hairy roots followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Independent hairy root RNAi lines; transgenic Arabidopsis lines; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cinnamic acid supplementation; MeJA had no significant effect on transcript level
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Spectrophotometric CoA ligase assay; HPLC and LC-MS metabolite profiling in RNAi roots and transient-expression tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Discussed as recruited from a CLL ancestor likely involved in fatty acid/jasmonate metabolism rather than canonical 4CL duplication; Arabidopsis expressing only SbCLL-7 did not accumulate pinocembrin, revealing need for SbCHS-2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cinnamic acid activation branch feeding the 4′-deoxyflavone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential competition with endogenous C4H for cinnamic acid; peroxisomal localization may constrain or channel flux
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Acyl-activating enzyme/4CL-like family; peroxisomal SKL motif
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Transcript in aerial organs at least threefold lower than roots; RNAi lines retained 48%, 25%, and 21% transcript of control; baicalin reduced to ~50% in line 8 and ~30% in line 2; wogonoside reduced from 11.10 to 5.05 and 4.62 μg mg−1 DW in lines 8 and 2; baicalein reduced from 10.30 to 3.51 and 1.43 μg mg−1 DW; kinetic parameters for cinnamic acid: Km 172 μM, Vmax 168 nkat mg−1, Vmax/Km 0.97, 27-fold and 9-fold higher efficiency than SbCLL-1 and SbCLL-5 respectively
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Pinocembrin and downstream root-specific 4′-deoxyflavones including chrysin, baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Hairy roots; roots, stems, leaves, flowers; Arabidopsis seedlings; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Variety_or_Cultivar: Arabidopsis ecotype Columbia used for heterologous validation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Limited direct value for specialized 4′-deoxyflavone engineering; may contribute to aerial tissue flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses one aromatic CoA ester with malonyl-CoA units to form chalcone scaffold in flavonoid biosynthesis; specific substrate preference not directly assayed in this study
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Uses malonyl-CoA and aromatic CoA ester substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Flower-enriched CHS isoform likely contributing to aerial/classic flavonoid production rather than root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression analysis, phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR organ profiling and MeJA response
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Flowers versus roots and other organs; MeJA-treated hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Annotated as CHS by sequence homology and conserved phylogenetic placement among plant CHS proteins; full-length cDNA cloned
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Very high expression in flowers but low in roots, opposite to root-specific flavone accumulation, indicating involvement in aerial/classic flavonoid branch rather than RSF branch
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq from flowers and hairy roots followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Expression profiling by RNA-seq/qRT-PCR; no direct metabolite linkage assay for this isoform
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Flower-specific isoform; 4038 raw reads in flowers versus 45 in hairy roots; separated relatively recently from SbCHS-2
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could compete for precursor flux away from the specialized root branch if ectopically expressed
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Chalcone intermediate (specific product not directly tested)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Aromatic CoA ester plus malonyl-CoA (likely 4-coumaroyl-CoA in classic branch)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase / chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: 4038 raw reads in flowers versus 45 in hairy roots; expression enhanced about 6-fold by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Potential effect on precursor partitioning rather than direct RSF accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important engineering target to produce pinocembrin/chrysin and downstream medicinal 4′-deoxyflavones in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form pinocembrin chalcone, which is then converted to pinocembrin (with endogenous or supplied CHI activity)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required, together with SbCLL-7, to generate pinocembrin and thus enable chrysin and downstream root-specific 4′-deoxyflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression analysis, phylogenetic analysis, qRT-PCR organ profiling, transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana with and without SbCHI and cinnamic acid supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Roots/hairy roots; MeJA-treated hairy roots; transiently expressed in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Coexpression of SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 in N. benthamiana produced detectable pinocembrin, demonstrating SbCHS-2 can use cinnamoyl-CoA in vivo; addition of SbFNSII-2 enabled chrysin formation
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Root-enriched expression and transient reconstruction show SbCHS-2 acts downstream of SbCLL-7 and upstream of SbFNSII-2 by generating pinocembrin precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq from flowers and hairy roots followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cinnamic acid supplementation; 100 μM methyl jasmonate for 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC metabolite profiling in transiently transformed tobacco leaves; RNA-seq/qRT-PCR for expression
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to previously reported SbCHS2a/SbCHS-C and SbCHS2b, likely alleles of same gene; close to SvCHS from S. viscidula, another RSF-producing species
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase / chalcone synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: 40887 raw reads in hairy roots versus 145 in flowers; transcript especially high in roots and very low in flowers; expression enhanced about 16-fold by MeJA; in tobacco, SbCLL-7 + SbCHS-2 yielded detectable pinocembrin, and addition of SbFNSII-2 yielded chrysin
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Hairy roots; roots, stems, leaves, flowers; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Specialized 4′-deoxyflavone/root-specific flavone (RSF) biosynthetic pathway and general flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Useful supporting enzyme for pathway reconstitution, though not a specialized control point; endogenous host CHI may substitute in engineering contexts
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes pinocembrin chalcone to pinocembrin; the study indicates this step may also occur spontaneously or via nonspecialized CHI
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports pinocembrin formation, but no specialized Scutellaria-specific CHI isoform was identified and it was not required for pathway reconstruction in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification, sequence comparison, transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana with or without SbCHI
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Hairy roots/roots and aerial tissues; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Annotated by sequence similarity to known S. baicalensis CHIs; inclusion in pathway reconstruction did not alter production relative to endogenous tobacco CHI, consistent with expected CHI activity
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Single CHI transcript found; tobacco reconstruction showed SbCLL-7 + SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin with or without SbCHI, placing CHI at the chalcone-to-pinocembrin step but indicating no specialized isoform is required
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: RNA-seq search for CHI homologs followed by full-length cDNA cloning
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cinnamic acid supplementation in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC metabolite profiling in transiently transformed tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Only a single CHI transcript was found; likely allelic to previously reported CHI sequences; authors conclude pinocembrin may be formed either spontaneously or through the common CHI active in aerial tissues
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Shared chalcone-to-flavanone step in both classic flavone and 4′-deoxyflavone branches
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In tobacco, SbCLL-7 + SbCHS-2 produced pinocembrin; addition of SbCHI gave similar pinocembrin production, indicating no measurable enhancement under assay conditions

Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis
